items.title: Push to axe Kanye West as headliner for London's Wireless Festival after antisemitic remarks
items.summary: There is increasing pressure for the rapper to be pulled as headliner of the Wireless Festival in London, as UK PM Keir Starmer weighs in and Pepsi withdraws as lead sponsor.

items.title: From Sway to Red Sunset: Bic Runga's return to music reflects where she's at
items.summary: "There were definitely times where I thought I was never going to make anything again," the New Zealand star says of new album Red Sunset.

items.title: Tim David stars in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's win over Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: It is a mixed day for the Australians in the Indian Premier League, with Tim David powering RCB to a victory, while Travis Head fails to make an impact in Sunrisers Hyderabad's loss in Lucknow.

items.title: Feral animals, biosecurity and careers on agenda for SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Rangers
items.summary: The first SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Ranger Conference has brought together those on the front line of the fight against feral animals and plants.

items.title: 'Easter bilby' populations quadruple across Australian sanctuaries
items.summary: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

items.title: Hundreds of flying fox pups starving to death in wake of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle
items.summary: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, with hundreds of the native bats dead or dying in Top End streets.

items.title: Commuters swap the car for e-bikes as fuel prices soar amid Middle East war
items.summary: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting a surge in sales.

items.title: Angry customers who lost millions in Zone RV collapse demand ASIC investigation
items.summary: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

items.title: Youth detention watchdog calls for upgrade to 'harmful' cells to improve children's access to justice
items.summary: South Australia's Youth Court cells do not meet "acceptable minimum standards of safety, dignity and care", resulting in child detainees increasingly appearing in court via video link, the state's youth detention watchdog says.

items.title: Easter tourism brings hope of economic recovery for fire-hit communities in Victoria
items.summary: Businesses in fire-affected communities of Victoria welcome holiday-makers and daytrippers, as the recovery continues.

items.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Threats tell us more about Trump's frustration than anything else
items.summary: It's day 37 of the war in the Middle East. The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Jerusalem, where Donald Trump's threats to obliterate Iran are starting to feel like business as usual.

items.title: Iran rejects ceasefire proposal as intelligence chief killed in US-Israeli strikes
items.summary: Iran says the intelligence chief of Iran's Revolutionary Guards, Major General Majid Khademi, was killed by US-Israeli strikes.

items.title: Showers for Queensland east coast as forecasters watch Tropical Cyclone Maila
items.summary: The majority of Queensland will enjoy mild weather this week, with scattered showers across the east coast, while further north, Cyclone Maila is set to intensify.

items.title: Dance classes providing connection, creativity and movement for older Australians
items.summary: Judy Taylor discovered dance later in life, but it has now become a source of joy, energy, and purpose.

items.title: Terminal diagnosis hasn't stopped the final exhibition for Joan Humble
items.summary: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.

items.title: How clean should a rental property be before moving in?
items.summary: Having to clean a new rental property before settling in is a common experience for renters, but landlords have obligations when it comes to rental cleanliness upon moving in.

items.title: Zac Lomax's false start with Force adds to anticipation ahead of Super Rugby Pacific debut
items.summary: Fans will have to wait for Zac Lomax's Western Force debut, providing further intrigue about how the ex-NRL star will cope when he takes his first steps in professional rugby.

items.title: Cape Grim's 50-year air record reveals true scale of humans' impact on planet
items.summary: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

items.title: Lebanese victim describes 45-minute Israeli attack in country's south, where Christian villages are under threat
items.summary: Bashir was injured when Israel attacked his home in a Christian village in Lebanon's south, where many residents refuse to leave despite threats of destruction.

items.title: As Iran charges for access to Strait of Hormuz, world faces dangerous new era of maritime tollbooths
items.summary: If Iran's idea of charging ships to pass through maritime choke points catches on, we are entering a new era of much more expensive, more dangerous global shipping.

items.title: This week in history quiz, April 6: Take a walk down memory lane

items.title: Fiji cruise ship runs aground after hitting reef near 'Cast Away island'

items.title: Fuel costs hit some farmers hard, forces families to change holiday travel plans

items.title: Bulldogs down plucky Bombers but lose two more players to injury

items.title: Rescuers save huge sea turtle discovered stranded upside down on Coffs Coast beach

items.title: 'Changing genders wasn't going to change how I felt about Kate'

items.title: Mount Gambier's 'Blue Blob' sculpture sparked controversy at first. How do locals feel about it now?

items.summary: Eagles coach Andrew McQualter leaps to Harley Reid's defence after his discipline came into question during the 128-point thrashing dished out by the Swans.

items.title: How often should you change your toilet brush?

items.title: Demons star Kysaiah Pickett praised after brilliant start to AFL season
items.summary: Ed Langdon says Melbourne teammate Kysaiah Pickett is flourishing in his midfield role, as the Demons continue life under new coach Steven King.
